item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, in the Marvel universe, mutant very specifically means someone whose powers are the result of a mutation at birth. I think you are right that metahuman is more a DC term though, perhaps superhuman would be better in this context.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Honestly, I loved this one. A great take on pataphysical entities, but I feel I have to let my geek flag fly a bit and point out that in the letter "sent" from S.H.I.E.L.D., it says Metahuman, but metahuman is a DC comics thing, while mutant is marvel.
It's not exactly important to the story of the scip, but if they are taking their sentence structure and the like straight from pataphysical universes, they would have said mutant. As I said, it's not exactly important to the file, but would help with continuity.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This made me very happy, you went a way that I didn't expect!

This is pretty much the exact reaction I was hoping for from the author of the conprocs, so I'm feeling good.

I think you probably spent too much text kind of grappling with an explanation of some concepts, kind of felt like you were exploring/determining them as you went. That could probably be tightened up if you wanted?

Definitely fair. Especially with the context paragraph, I was really struggling to think how I was going to incorporate it into the article, to the point that the dilemma was becoming a barrier to me getting started, so I just threw it out near the front. I actually wasn't figuring that paragraph out as I wrote it, I already had that idea of the pataphysical mechanism as part of my headcanon.

If you have ideas on how to improve it, I certainly welcome them after the contest is over.

item.title: Re: Hmm.
item.content:encoded:
I'm very glad to see there are at least parts of this you liked, I was really looking forward to seeing what you thought.

I will never understand the premise, reason, or humor behind Ooo ooo etc. and this just confounds things even more.

Fair. I loved it, but initially thought of it as a novelty that couldn't really be incorporated into a normal conception of the Foundationverse. But when I thought of tying it in with this I was excited, because I could contextualize it in my canon in a sensible way. Even better, the concept it introduced of using narrative devices as weapons inspired a lot of ideas for the -2 conflicts, so it was a big help for me.

The -2 instances feel too… easy, for lack of a better term. With the exception of the last one, they're the equivalent of Skill Books in Skyrim, and that just bugs me because we're told what they had to go through to get that knowledge without actually seeing it for ourselves.

Extremely fair. Early on I knew that one longer account of a -2 quest would be better and more narratively satisfying, but I didn't have any ideas that I knew how to stretch to that length, and didn't think I was talented enough to come up with events or prose as clever or interesting as, say, what Tanhony did in Ooo x7 in the time I had. I went with the format of several quick descriptions of events because I felt that was more doable, and hoped that introducing a wide variety of ideas and neat thematic ties to other works would be interesting enough to work as the meat of the article. If it didn't work for some, that's on me.

This feels like what I've been afraid Pataphysics is going to turn into, but also what might have been its inevitable conclusion— a vehicle for fanfiction. Take away the (admittedly well-done) tie-ins to other media, and you're not left with much.

Well, I don't think this exactly counts as Fanfiction, they never actually enter a fictional universe. But I do see how you could see it as a stepping stone to it. I'm glad you thought the tie-ins were well done! But you are correct that they are the meat of the article, I don't see this as a negative, but I can see how someone else would.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Similar to Ihp’s vote, my feelings on this are conflicted, though my problems lie more with the structure of the SCP. In terms of stuff I did like, I think the idea is very creative, and it’s well-written. I know some may have a problem with the Pataphysics Department stuff, but it never got too “wink wink, we’re not real” for me to not care about it. Part of the problem of Pataphysics is that, at some point, if you’re reminded too much that it’s all fictional, you just… stop caring. This neatly side-steps this by focusing on the people between narrative dimensions, which allows for the reader to actually care about the semi-fictional entities.

My issues lie in how the skip is laid out. Firstly, the Description reveals all of its cards too early for my tastes. I’d rather the description at the beginning describe SCP-5875-1 solely (along with at least one example of an SCP-5875-1 instance besides just the last one). Then, after the description, we see the final note, then the first SCP-5875-2 instance, followed by a description of SCP-5875-2 and the remaining instances. This would be a more engaging narrative than just describing everything in Description, and it would allow you to show a bit more than tell.

Speaking of SCP-5875-2, while they are well-written, I’m not sure they escalate in an interesting way. At a certain point, I was just like “yeah, I get it”, and I don’t really have a specific understanding of SCP-5875-A culture. I’d rather the logs be in a more linear order, with earlier logs perhaps hinting at the presence of powerful entities in later logs. I’d also maybe like an insight into SCP-5875-A culture and daily life. Maybe one of the logs is a common robbery, and we learn about how SCP-5875-A stores work or whatever. As of now, they’re all a bit samey.

As I’m opposed to the no-vote, I’ll give this a downvote for now, but I think this has great potential, and you are a way confident writer, so I think you can make it work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It drags on a little too long without variation and I don't have any connection with the fictional organizations presented; not sure if there's anything for me here. Everything just feels kind of… "wall of text"-y.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh shit, these were my conprocs! Sorry!

This made me very happy, you went a way that I didn't expect!

A few thoughts:

+ show block
– hide block

When an individual conceives of a non-real scenario, an information packet representing that scenario emanates out of their universe and travels downwards in the pataphysical dimension before attaching itself to a nascent universe with low information content, bringing about the imagined scenario in that universe.

Well, that's terrifying.

I think you probably spent too much text kind of grappling with an explanation of some concepts, kind of felt like you were exploring/determining them as you went. That could probably be tightened up if you wanted? I'm sorry I wasn't able to give feedback before this, but if you're interested after the contest I'd be happy to. Especially since some of that was my fault. I mean, your call, I still enjoyed it.

The hero's journey was a great take!

I liked them "beating" 2747, had me worried for a minute there.

+1 obviously :)

item.title: Hmm.
item.content:encoded:
I've been ruminating on how to phrase this post for the last ten minutes. I… honestly don't know how to feel about it.

There are some good parts here— a lot of this gives me a Thursday Next vibe, which is admittedly a massive influence on my work in pataphysics, and more tie-ins to Site-87 will always be appreciated.

But a lot of things sour it for me.

  • I will never understand the premise, reason, or humor behind Ooo ooo etc. and this just confounds things even more.
  • The -2 instances feel too… easy, for lack of a better term. With the exception of the last one, they're the equivalent of Skill Books in Skyrim, and that just bugs me because we're told what they had to go through to get that knowledge without actually seeing it for ourselves.
  • This feels like what I've been afraid Pataphysics is going to turn into, but also what might have been its inevitable conclusion— a vehicle for fanfiction. Take away the (admittedly well-done) tie-ins to other media, and you're not left with much.

I'm going to have to chew this over a bit before I make my decision. For now, +-0

This is really well done, IMO. As someone who has been doing their own head in trying to write a Unified Theory of Pataphysics, this sits very well with my idea of the kinds of stories and anomalies that should exist in this department. A reality 'below' us possesses less narrative dimension, which forces these entities to be much more tied to narrative structure than in the Foundation baseline.

Would've upvoted even without the mystery at the end, but that solidified it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Alright, in the nick of time, the contest is entered. I thought this would be a nice way to explain how I think pataphysical creation works while not being ABOUT that, and giving a fun new space for pataphysics to explore.

Big thanks to:
fairydoctor, cybersqyd, and Auxiphor for their valuable feedback.
Ihp for their contributions to pataphysics concepts on the site which were valuable for me in this work.
Tanhony, whose brilliant creation Cowboy Hephaestus and The Fuck Patrol was a valuable inspiration and influence for this work.
And of course, to my mystery coauthor.

My author page.
